title: Move native LICHEN BLE behind app owner
description: Refactor native BLE UART/NUS so advertising and connection callback ownership are provided by the shared BLE app owner while preserving SLIP-framed IPv6 payload behavior and the existing public ble_uart_send_slip() contract.
acceptance_criteria: native_sim tests or build coverage prove native NUS/SLIP behavior still resets RX state on connect/disconnect and sends notifications through the owner-managed connection; NUS UUID collision with MeshCore remains explicitly blocked.
notes:
Implemented native BLE UART ownership migration for no-hardware scope. ble_app_owner now owns the single active BLE connection, registers the shared BT_CONN_CB_DEFINE callback, exposes ble_app_owner_conn_ref()/ble_app_owner_conn_unref(), stores per-surface connected/disconnected callbacks, suppresses stale disconnects that do not match the owner-held connection, and clears owner connection refs on stop/reset. Native BLE UART now registers its callbacks through ble_app_owner, no longer stores a private s_conn, sends SLIP notifications through ble_app_owner_conn_ref(), resets RX state on owner-dispatched connect/disconnect, and requires BT_MAX_CONN=1 to match the current single-session owner model.

Tests added in lichen/tests/ble_app_owner: native init now asserts owner-registered callbacks; native connection test uses fake non-null bt_conn tokens under CONFIG_ZTEST, verifies connect resets RX state and creates an owner-held connection, verifies direct owner ref/unref counts, executes ble_uart_send_slip() while connected through a fake GATT MTU/notify backend and asserts the exact escaped SLIP notification payload, verifies stale/non-owned disconnect does not reset RX or restart advertising, verifies matched disconnect clears connection and restarts advertising, and verifies disconnected send still returns -ENOTCONN.

Reviews: initial Nietzsche/Poincare reviews found P2 stale disconnect dispatch and P2 missing connected-send coverage; fixed with active-connection matching, BT_MAX_CONN=1, owner-held connection refs, and a connected send test. Focused Aristotle/Curie reviews then found a P1 stale test helper typo and a P3 multi-notify chunking coverage gap. Fixed the P1 by replacing the old ble_app_owner_test_conn_unref() call with public ble_app_owner_conn_unref(). Final Bernoulli/Darwin reviews found no P0/P1/P2; Darwin retained only P3 multi-notify chunking coverage. Created follow-up project-LICHEN-t2hn.22.2.1 for that coverage gap.

Local/static check: git diff --check passed; rg confirmed no ble_app_owner_test_conn_unref references remain.
